microsoft_ft: Architected Azure's **recovery validation platform** from a 2-person operation into a self-service system, standardizing deployment patterns to sustain 45+ annual resilience drills across enterprise accounts including Databricks, Walmart, SAP, and NetApp.

Pioneered Azure's first **proactive resilience testing** capability — designed the rack-level drill architecture, mapped regulated-environment constraints, and delivered a 94% recovery rate program in 4 months, surfacing critical hardware defects.

Directed **14 cross-org recovery executions** under executive visibility, including serving as bridge lead for a sovereign-cloud network isolation test tied to a $1.5B+ enterprise contract requiring strict governance and compliance controls.

Translated recurring field blockers into **reusable accelerators**, authoring runbooks and reference architectures across CI/CD and platform-recovery workflows adopted by multiple account and engineering teams.

Built an **internal AI agent** for drill planning, restructuring discovery and workflow-activation cycles to cut planning time by 39% and increase drill capacity by 21% — turning a one-off prototype into a durable field asset.

Navigated complex stakeholder environments across security, compliance, and platform-engineering teams to **align adoption strategies** and convert ambiguous enterprise constraints into concrete architecture decisions and phased execution plans.
microsoft_2023: Championed **AI workflow adoption** for code-generation tooling across Azure, conducting technical discovery with 14 service teams to surface activation blockers and delivering enablement sessions that saved 37 engineering hours monthly.

Influenced the **product roadmap** toward intent-based YAML generation by leading 11+ structured discovery interviews with Azure service teams, translating ambiguous user pain into prioritized design briefs Engineering could act on directly.

Accelerated knowledge retrieval by migrating documentation to an **AI-powered semantic search** platform with rigorous metadata standards, cutting lookup time by 83% and producing a reusable adoption pattern for downstream teams.

Conducted structured discovery with 20+ service teams to **surface 81 hours of manual toil** per region launch, then built a roadmap to automate critical paths — converting field findings into repeatable technical guidance for platform engineering.

Engineered a **Power BI dashboard** tracking operational toil across 140+ teams, giving leadership visibility into automation gaps and enabling data-driven prioritization of the highest-impact platform investments.
amazon_robotics: Achieved **zero operational downtime** across a 2,000+ unit pilot by architecting the legacy OS migration strategy, mapping platform dependencies across 1,200+ stations, and authoring a reusable deployment runbook for future rollouts.

Facilitated Agile ceremonies and **prioritized the engineering backlog** to resolve 40+ high-severity tickets, stabilizing the developer intake process and unblocking CI/CD pipeline work across IT, Operations, and Engineering teams.

Drove cross-functional alignment to implement **automated CI/CD pipelines**, accelerating the software deployment cycle by 25% and establishing a repeatable deployment pattern reused across subsequent robotics platform releases.
pro_painters: Increased job bookings by **26%** by redesigning the end-to-end proposal lifecycle — building a structured CRM workflow to manage 200+ monthly scoping and invoicing records and eliminate adoption blockers across the sales team.

Executed a digital-first **go-to-market strategy** optimizing the website and Google profile, reducing Customer Acquisition Cost by 13% and lifting conversion rates by 2.7% through data-driven channel prioritization.

Conducted profitability analysis across **1,000+ project records** to identify margin leaks, delivering a pricing-model recommendation that gave leadership a repeatable framework for scoping high-value work.

tailoring_notes: 'Reusable accelerators / deployment patterns / reference architectures' (JD core term) woven into microsoft_ft bullets 3, 5 and amazon_robotics bullet 1 — replacing generic 'automation' language with GitLab FDE asset vocabulary.

'Structured technical discovery' (JD term under What you'll do) woven into microsoft_2023 bullet 2 and microsoft_2022 bullet 2 — framing user-research work as discovery that produces product briefs Engineering can act on.

'Regulated-environment / governance controls / sovereign-cloud' (JD preferred quals + examples of work) surfaced in microsoft_ft bullets 3 and 4 — directly mirroring GitLab's regulated-deployment and compliance language.
'AI workflow activation' (JD Performance Indicator term) used in microsoft_2023 bullet 1 — reframing Copilot adoption work as workflow-activation discovery, matching GitLab DAP adoption framing.

'Convert field findings into repeatable technical guidance' (JD What you'll do) woven into microsoft_2022 bullet 2 — translating the toil-discovery work into the FDE asset-creation narrative GitLab explicitly calls out.
